page: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v5 is a compute-focused virtual machine instance belonging to the DLDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This low-memory architecture is paired with 3686 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. The CPU architecture is x86_64, incorporating advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 93258.02 and a Passmark CPU score of 76019.64. Cryptographic performance is particularly notable, achieving a top-tier rating in the Passmark CPU encryption test at 86381.58. Memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 328.17 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 5005.65 GB/sec for cached small-block reads. While single-core performance remains in the average tier, multi-threaded workloads like bzip3 decompression and software compilation perform strongly.

The primary tradeoff of the D96lds_v5 is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, which makes it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or massive in-memory caching layers. However, this resource distribution offers high cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads that do not require large memory footprints. The inclusion of 3686 GB of local storage provides high-capacity, low-latency workspace. This profile is well-suited for multi-threaded batch processing, software compilation, web serving, and moderate-scale database operations.
description: The Microsoft Azure D96lds_v5 is a compute-focused virtual machine from the DLDSv5 family, powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs (48 physical cores), 192.0 GB of RAM, and 3686 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. Benchmarks show strong multi-core performance, including high memory bandwidth and top-tier encryption capabilities, alongside average single-core speeds. With a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, the instance offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy tasks that do not demand massive RAM. It is highly suited for multi-threaded batch processing, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v5 is a compute-focused instance with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 3686 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core performance and top-tier cryptographic execution.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96lds_v5 features 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 3686 GB local storage, optimized for compute-heavy, multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-core compute instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and substantial local storage for parallelized, CPU-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor.
192.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU resource ratio.
3686 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Strong multi-core performance in software compilation and cryptographic operations.
Optimized for multi-threaded batch processing, web serving, and database workloads.
